codeReview.summary: Feature implemented correctly as documentation-only work. Created docs/OH-MY-PI.md (291 lines) covering oh-my-pi extension-based provider registration using pi.registerProvider() with openai-completions API type. The guide includes prerequisites, step-by-step configuration, all 45 Cursor Pro model definitions with proper schema (reasoning, contextWindow, maxTokens, cost), testing endpoints, troubleshooting, advanced config, and architecture diagram. Follows the established OPENCODE.md documentation pattern exactly.

issues.description: Handoff claims 'All 47 Cursor Pro models' but the extension code only lists 45 models (excluding the llama2 in the ollama example). This is a minor verbal discrepancy — the actual model count in the cursor-acp provider is 45, not 47. No functional impact since all actual Cursor models are included.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed the established docs pattern correctly (matching OPENCODE.md structure). No convention violations detected.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: docs/OH-MY-PI.md mirrors the sections, style, and format of docs/OPENCODE.md

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker fetched oh-my-pi's GitHub repo and custom-provider.md documentation before writing the guide, confirming the pi.registerProvider() API approach. skillFeedback.followedProcedure is true with no deviations.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows FetchUrl calls to github.com/can1357/oh-my-pi and github.com/badlogic/pi-mono/blob/main/packages/coding-agent/docs/custom-provider.md. Handoff shows skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The library/environment.md documents cursor-agent prerequisites but does not mention oh-my-pi. The library/architecture.md mentions oh-my-pi as a known client but provides no integration details. The worker's research into oh-my-pi's extension system (pi.registerProvider, openai-completions API type, ExtensionAPI type import) produced knowledge that would be useful for future workers — consider whether to add a brief oh-my-pi section to environment.md.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: environment.md has no oh-my-pi reference. Worker spent tool calls fetching oh-my-pi docs to understand the extension API.

sharedStateObservations.observation: No new services or commands were introduced that would need to be added to services.yaml. The proxy service (port 32124) was already documented.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: services.yaml unchanged. The feature is documentation-only.

summary: Pass. Feature created comprehensive oh-my-pi integration documentation (docs/OH-MY-PI.md) correctly following the OPENCODE.md pattern. The extension code uses the oh-my-pi pi.registerProvider() API with openai-completions correctly. All prerequisites, config steps, model definitions, testing endpoints, and troubleshooting are documented. bun test (71 tests) and tsc passed. One minor verbal discrepancy: handoff says 47 models but 45 are listed (llama2 in the ollama example is not a Cursor model). No blocking issues.
